content_id:
[Kembali ke daftar isi] [81] Baihaqi dan Hakim, yang menyatakannya sahih dan memang demikian adanya. Juga memiliki hadis penguat yang diriwayatkan oleh sepuluh sahabatnya: diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Asakir (17/202/2). Lihat Irwa (354). CATATAN: Dua hadis ini menunjukkan bahwa sunnah adalah menetapkan pandangan pada tempat sujud di tanah, sehingga tindakan sebagian orang yang shalat dengan menutup mata selama shalat adalah bentuk kesalehan yang salah arah, karena sebaik-baik petunjuk adalah petunjuk Muhammad shallallahu alaihi wa sallam. [82] ibid. [83] Abu Dawud dan Ahmad dengan isnad yang sahih (Irwa, 1771); yang dimaksud dengan "Rumah" di sini adalah Kabah, sebagaimana konteks hadis ini menunjukkan. [84] Bukhari dan Abu Dawud. [85] Bukhari, Muslim, dan Siraj. [86] Tirmizi dan Hakim, yang menyatakannya sahih, lihat Sahih at-Targhib (no. 353) [87] Bukhari dan Abu Dawud. [88] Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dan lainnya. Ibn Khuzaimah dan Ibn Hibban menyatakannya sahih. Lihat Sahih at-Targhib (no. 555) [89] Ahmad dan Abu Yala. Lihat Sahih at-Targhib (no. 556). [90] Mukhlis dalam Ahadis Muntaqah, Tabarani, Ruyani, Diya dalam al-Mukhtarah, Ibn Majah, Ahmad, dan Ibn Asakir. Haitami menyatakannya sahih dalam Asna al-Matalib. [91] Muslim. [92] Pakaian wol yang memiliki tanda-tanda. [93] Pakaian kasar tanpa tanda-tanda. [94] Bukhari, Muslim, dan Malik. Disebutkan dalam Irwa (376). [95] "Sebuah ruangan kecil yang tertanam sedikit di dalam tanah, seperti kamar kecil atau lemari" (Nihayah). [96] Bukhari, Muslim, dan Abu Awanah. Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam tidak memerintahkan penghapusan atau perobekan gambar-gambar tersebut tetapi hanya menyingkirkannya karena — dan Allah lebih mengetahui — gambar-gambar itu bukanlah gambar makhluk bernyawa. Buktinya adalah bahwa beliau shallallahu alaihi wa sallam pernah menyobek gambar-gambar lain sebagaimana terbukti dari banyak riwayat dalam Bukhari dan Muslim, dan siapa pun yang ingin

mengeksplorasi ini lebih lanjut sebaiknya mengkonsultasikan Fath al-Baari (10/321) dan Gayat al-Maram fi Takhrij Ahadis al-Halal wal-Haram (nos. 131-145). [97] Bukhari dan Muslim.
content_en:
[Back to contents] [81] Baihaqi and Haakim, who declared it sahih and it is as he said. It also has a strengthening hadith reported by ten of his Companions: transmitted by Ibn `Asaakir (17/202/2). See Irwaa' (354). *NB: These two ahaadeeth show that the sunnah is to fix one's sight on the place of prostration on the ground, so the action of some worshippers of closing their eyes during Prayer is misdirected piety, for the best guidance is the guidance of Muhammad (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam). [82] ibid. [83] Abu Daawood & Ahmad with a sahih isnaad (Irwaa', 1771); what is meant here by `the House' is the Ka'bah, as the context of this hadith shows. [84] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [85] Bukhaari, Muslim & Siraaj. [86] Tirmidhi & Haakim, who declared it sahih, cf. sahih at-Targheeb (no. 353) [87] Bukhaari and Abu Daawood. [88] Transmitted by Abu Daawood and others. Ibn Khuzaimah and Ibn Hibbaan declared it sahih. See sahih at-Targheeb (no.555) [89] Ahmad and Abu Ya`laa. See sahih at-Targheeb (no. 556). [90] Mukhlis in Ahaadeeth Muntaqaah, Tabaraani, Rooyaani, Diyaa' in al-Mukhtaarah, Ibn Maajah, Ahmad and Ibn `Asaakir. Haitami declared it sahih in Asnaa al-Mataalib. [91] Muslim. [92] A woollen garment having marks. [93] A coarse garment without marks. [94] Bukhaari , Muslim & Maalik. It is given in Irwaa' (376). [95] "A small room embedded in the ground slightly, like a small chamber or cupboard" (Nihaayah). [96] Bukhaari , Muslim & Abu `Awaanah. The Messenger (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam) did not order the wiping out or tearing of the pictures but only removed them because - and Allaah knows best - they were not pictures of things having souls. The evidence for this is that he (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam) tore other pictures as proved by many narrations in Bukhaari and Muslim, and whoever wishes to

explore this further should consult Fath al-Baari (10/321) and Ghaayah al-Maraam fi Takhreej Ahaadeeth al-Halaal wal-Haraam (nos. 131-145). [97] Bukhaari and Muslim.
content_ar: [Back to contents] [81] Baihaqi and Haakim, who declared it صحيح and it is as قال. It أيضاً has a strengthening الحديث reported by ten of his الصحابة: transmitted by Ibn `Asaakir (17/202/2). See Irwaa' (354). *NB: These two ahaadeeth show that the السنة is to fix one's sight on the place of prostration on the ground, لذا the action of some worshippers of closing their eyes خلال الصلاة is misdirected piety, for the best الهداية is the الهداية of محمد ﷺ (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam). [82] ibid. [83] Abu Daawood & أحمد with a صحيح isnaad (Irwaa', 1771); what is meant here by `the House' is the الكعبة المشرفة, as the context of this الحديث shows. [84] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [85] Bukhaari, مسلم & Siraaj. [86] الترمذي & Haakim, who declared it صحيح, cf. صحيح at-Targheeb (no. 353) [87] Bukhaari and Abu Daawood. [88] Transmitted by Abu Daawood and others. Ibn Khuzaimah and Ibn Hibbaan declared it صحيح. See صحيح at-Targheeb (no.555) [89] أحمد and Abu Ya`laa. See صحيح at-Targheeb (no. 556). [90] Mukhlis in Ahaadeeth Muntaqaah, Tabaraani, Rooyaani, Diyaa' in al-Mukhtaarah, Ibn Maajah, أحمد and Ibn `Asaakir. Haitami declared it صحيح in Asnaa al-Mataalib. [91] مسلم. [92] A woollen garment having marks. [93] A coarse garment without marks. [94] Bukhaari , مسلم & Maalik. It is given in Irwaa' (376). [95] "A small room embedded in the ground slightly, like a small chamber or cupboard" (Nihaayah). [96] Bukhaari , مسلم & Abu `Awaanah. الرسول ﷺ (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam) did not order the wiping out or tearing of the pictures but only removed them لأن - and Allaah knows best - they were not pictures of things having النفوس. The evidence for this is that he (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam) tore other pictures as proved by many narrations in Bukhaari and مسلم, and whoever wishes to explore this further should consult Fath al-Baari (10/321) and Ghaayah al-Maraam fi Takhreej Ahaadeeth al-Halaal wal-Haraam (nos. 131-145). [97] Bukhaari and مسلم.
